lines.original: Romeo
lines.translated: Romeo
lines.note: Transliteration: Romeo. Persona/label for a charming/romantic man; literal transfer.

lines.original: Chaali bhar ke naina mein chhori maut ka samaan
lines.translated: The girl's eyes are loaded with the stuff of death.
lines.note: Transliteration: Chaali bhar ke naina mein chhori maut ka samaan. 'chhori' = girl. Conservative reading kept ('loaded with the stuff of death').

lines.original: Kare kabje dilaan pe, bani phire Taliban
lines.translated: He seizes hearts — roaming around like a Taliban.
lines.note: Transliteration: Kare kabje dilaan pe, bani phire Taliban. 'dilaan' = hearts. Kept literal metaphor of fear/dominance.

lines.original: Bole haulihauli, bina goli maare kheeche jaan
lines.translated: They whisper softly — they take lives without firing a bullet.
lines.note: Transliteration: Bole haulihauli, bina goli maare kheeche jaan. Could be literal threat or figurative (charm); translated conservatively as killing without shooting.

lines.original: Chhore zinda laash bane, shehar banya shamshaan
lines.translated: The guys became living corpses; the city turned into a crematorium.
lines.note: Transliteration: Chhore zinda laash bane, shehar banya shamshaan. 'zinda laash' = living corpse; literal metaphor kept.

lines.original: Chaali bhar ke naina mein chhori maut ka samaan
lines.translated: The girl's eyes are loaded with the stuff of death.
lines.note: Transliteration: Chaali bhar ke naina mein chhori maut ka samaan. 'chhori' = girl. Conservative reading kept ('loaded with the stuff of death').

lines.original: Kare kabje dilaan pe, bani phire Taliban
lines.translated: He seizes hearts — roaming around like a Taliban.
lines.note: Transliteration: Kare kabje dilaan pe, bani phire Taliban. 'dilaan' = hearts. Kept literal metaphor of fear/dominance.

lines.original: Bole haulihauli, bina goli maare kheeche jaan
lines.translated: They whisper softly — they take lives without firing a bullet.
lines.note: Transliteration: Bole haulihauli, bina goli maare kheeche jaan. Could be literal threat or figurative (charm); translated conservatively as killing without shooting.

lines.original: Chhore zinda laash bane, shehar banya shamshaan
lines.translated: The guys became living corpses; the city turned into a crematorium.
lines.note: Transliteration: Chhore zinda laash bane, shehar banya shamshaan. 'zinda laash' = living corpse; literal metaphor kept.

lines.original: Saara jagg liya thug, phaink husan ka jaala
lines.translated: I took the whole world — I threw a net of beauty.
lines.transliteration: Tera chehra nasha maaf kare Mexico aala
lines.note: Transliteration: Saara jagg liya thug, phaink husan ka jaala. 'phaink ... jaala' = cast a net; translated conservatively.

lines.original: Tera chehra nasha maaf kare Mexico aala
lines.translated: Your face's intoxication — even Mexico would forgive.
lines.transliteration: Dil cheer ke gaye hain, teer tikkhe eyebrow
lines.note: Transliteration: Tera chehra nasha maaf kare Mexico aala. Conservative literal rendering used despite cultural ambiguity.

lines.original: Dil cheer ke gaye hain, teer tikkhe eyebrow
lines.translated: They ripped hearts apart — eyebrows sharp as arrows.
lines.transliteration: Beauty natural teri, poora face pe glow
lines.note: Transliteration: Dil cheer ke gaye hain, teer tikkhe eyebrow. 'teer tikkhe' = sharp like arrows; kept imagery intact.

lines.original: Beauty natural teri, poora face pe glow
lines.translated: Your beauty's natural, your whole face is glowing.
lines.note: Transliteration: Beauty natural teri, poora face pe glow. Straightforward, literal-modern mixing of English words handled directly.

lines.original: Reel dekhdekh teri main muka di saari GB
lines.translated: Watching your reels, I burned through all my GB.
lines.note: Transliteration: Reel dekhdekh teri main muka di saari GB. 'muka di saari GB' interpreted as using up data while watching reels.

lines.original: Dil ban ya trophy, re, tu bani RCB
lines.translated: Be it my heart or my trophy — you turned into RCB.
lines.note: Transliteration: Dil ban ya trophy, re, tu bani RCB. Kept literal RCB reference rather than inventing metaphor.

lines.original: Aankh shiny, step chhori kar rahi ae katt
lines.translated: Her eyes are shining, the girl’s got a killer step.
lines.note: Transliteration: Aankh shiny, step chhori kar rahi ae katt. 'katt' rendered as 'killer' for lyrical flow and likely meaning.

lines.original: Meri groza mein barood, dekh baahar aa reya butt
lines.translated: There's gunpowder in my Groza — look, the butt's sticking out.
lines.note: Transliteration: Meri groza mein barood, dekh baahar aa reya butt. 'Groza' = gun model; rendered accordingly.

lines.original: Note itne ke shehar tere peedan pe ni patte
lines.translated: So many banknotes that your city's trees don't have leaves.
lines.note: Transliteration: Note itne ke shehar tere peedan pe ni patte. Preserves metaphor of abundance of cash.

lines.original: Pehrun aaj bhi main yaaran gera maange hoye latte
lines.translated: Even today I still wear the pair my friends begged me for.
lines.note: Transliteration: Pehrun aaj bhi main yaaran gera maange hoye latte. 'latte' rendered conservatively as 'pair' (shoes/kicks).

lines.original: Age athaara te dhare hain sir maamle sangeen
lines.translated: Past eighteen, there are cases on heads; the matters are serious.
lines.note: Transliteration: Age athaara te dhare hain sir maamle sangeen. Refers to age 18 and legal/trouble cases (dhare = charges/records).

lines.original: Raakhan aise hathiyar, gale dodo magazine
lines.translated: We carry weapons like that — two magazines hanging round the neck.
lines.note: Magazine = ammunition clip; visible extra magazines around the neck.

lines.original: Road baap ka maan ke lyaanva sadkaan pe gaadi
lines.translated: Proud like the boss of the road, we bring our car out on the streets.
lines.note: 'baap' used as 'boss/king'; 'lyaanva' rendered conservatively as 'bring/bring out'.

lines.original: Bahar karaan kaam jinki ni ghar mein ajaadi
lines.translated: We do outside work for those whose homes aren't free.
lines.note: Conservative reading: performing 'outside' work on behalf of people lacking freedom at home.

lines.original: Leke chaale bade daale, gear marji ke ghale
lines.translated: We move with big stacks, shifting gears however we want.
lines.note: 'bade daale' = large sums/stacks; 'gear' likely literal or metaphor for tempo/control.

lines.original: Aage bamhana ka chhora, paachhe baithe hain do laale
lines.translated: There's a 'bamhana' boy up front, two lads sitting behind him.
lines.note: Kept 'bamhana' as-is because meaning isn't clear from context.

lines.original: Jutti jhaad ke rakhein hain, bairi saad ke
lines.translated: We've kicked off their shoes, to humiliate the enemy.
lines.note: Interprets the action as an insult/humiliation performed on enemies.

lines.original: Hala dyan jote naadke re beecho beech paad ke
lines.translated: We tie up Hala's shoes and put them right in the middle.
lines.note: High uncertainty; translation kept literal pieces rather than inventing meaning.

lines.original: Raakhe do numberi aa sandh, mora kar dena band
lines.translated: He keeps two shady ones with him — stop the show.
lines.note: 'do-numberi' = two-faced/shady people; 'mora' read as 'act/show' to fit tone.

lines.original: Bore batti raakhe khet ke main gaad ke
lines.translated: I plant the fuse/light in the field.
lines.note: Possible violent implication kept but wording conservative due to ambiguity.

lines.original: Khaanvaana churma, re, jama desi baane mein milenge
lines.translated: We'll eat churma — we'll meet up in our desi way.
lines.note: Churma = Punjabi sweet; 'jama desi baane' = meeting in a traditional/desi manner.

lines.original: Dand maarde akhaade mein, na to thaane mein milenge
lines.translated: We beat folks in the akhada; otherwise you'll run into us at the police station.
lines.note: 'akhada' = fighting ring/wrestling arena; also implies physical confrontation leading to police.

lines.original: Tanne jitni quality ginaayi ae main, chhori
lines.translated: Girl, you've counted how much quality I have.
lines.note: Chhori = affectionate address to a girl.

lines.original: Tanne aise chhore bas Haryane mein milenge
lines.translated: You'll only find boys like this in Haryana.
lines.note: Regional identity (Haryana) emphasized as source of the speaker's type.

lines.original: Maal baba ki daya te, nyu ae chaalenge sapaate
lines.translated: By Maal Baba's grace, we'll walk like this.
lines.note: Conservative rendering avoids speculating on the exact identity of 'Maal Baba'.

lines.original: Set kare ve trend jyon ni kise pe bhi paate
lines.translated: They set trends like nobody else can.
lines.note: Clear boast about trendsetting.

lines.original: Arey, bas kar tai aale dukhaan ki na puchhe
lines.translated: Hey, stop it — don't ask about those shops.
lines.note: 'dukhaan' = shops/joints; tone is dismissive/censoring curiosity.

lines.original: Log phaansi kha jya karein seh reya jitne main ghaate
lines.translated: People would be hanged for the losses I suffered.
lines.note: Figurative expression implying extreme hardship/penalty tied to speaker's losses.

lines.original: Tere naam chadhwa dyun, billo, AK santaali
lines.translated: I'll put an AK in your name, babe.
lines.note: 'billo' = affectionate address to a woman; the line implies gifting/registering a weapon under her name.

lines.original: Tanne goliyan ki kholan ki bana ke de doon baali
lines.translated: I'll make you a box of bullets and give it to you, girl.
lines.note: Maintains violent/braggadocious offer; phrasing chosen for naturalness.

lines.original: Tanne apni bana leyan, tere gel ki ne saali
lines.translated: I'll make you mine — your girl will be my 'saali'.
lines.note: Kept 'saali' untranslated with brief parenthetical in previous drafts; here preserved in quotes to flag cultural meaning.

lines.original: Taur itni bana dyaan, kade jaani ni sambhaali
lines.translated: I'll put on such an attitude that no one has ever managed it.
lines.note: transliteration: Taur itni bana dyaan, kade jaani ni sambhaali. 'taur' = attitude/stance.

lines.original: Ke ke karoon main bayaan, uthe dil mein toofan
lines.translated: How can I describe it — there's a storm in my heart.
lines.note: transliteration: Ke ke karoon main bayaan, uthe dil mein toofan.

lines.original: Reh wah chup bhi ni ho rahi, meri khule na zubaan
lines.translated: I can't stay quiet — my mouth won't keep shut.
lines.note: transliteration: Reh wah chup bhi ni ho rahi, meri khule na zubaan.

lines.original: Chaali bhar ke naina mein chhori maut ka samaan
lines.translated: The girl's eyes are loaded with the stuff of death.
lines.note: Transliteration: Chaali bhar ke naina mein chhori maut ka samaan. 'chhori' = girl. Conservative reading kept ('loaded with the stuff of death').

lines.original: Kare kabje dilaan pe, bani phire Taliban
lines.translated: He seizes hearts — roaming around like a Taliban.
lines.note: Transliteration: Kare kabje dilaan pe, bani phire Taliban. 'dilaan' = hearts. Kept literal metaphor of fear/dominance.

lines.note: Transliteration: Bole haulihauli, bina goli maare kheeche jaan. Could be literal threat or figurative (charm); translated conservatively as killing without shooting.

lines.original: Chhore zinda laash bane, shehar banya shamshaan

lines.note: Transliteration: Chhore zinda laash bane, shehar banya shamshaan. 'zinda laash' = living corpse; literal metaphor kept.

lines.translated: The girl's eyes are loaded with the stuff of death.
lines.note: Transliteration: Chaali bhar ke naina mein chhori maut ka samaan. 'chhori' = girl. Conservative reading kept ('loaded with the stuff of death').

lines.original: Kare kabje dilaan pe, bani phire Taliban
lines.translated: He seizes hearts — roaming around like a Taliban.
lines.note: Transliteration: Kare kabje dilaan pe, bani phire Taliban. 'dilaan' = hearts. Kept literal metaphor of fear/dominance.

lines.original: Bole haulihauli, bina goli maare kheeche jaan
lines.translated: They whisper softly — they take lives without firing a bullet.
lines.note: Transliteration: Bole haulihauli, bina goli maare kheeche jaan. Could be literal threat or figurative (charm); translated conservatively as killing without shooting.

lines.original: Chhore zinda laash bane, shehar banya shamshaan
lines.translated: The guys became living corpses; the city turned into a crematorium.
lines.note: Transliteration: Chhore zinda laash bane, shehar banya shamshaan. 'zinda laash' = living corpse; literal metaphor kept.

lines.original: Nyoliwala this side, baby
lines.translated: Nyoliwala's on this side, baby.
lines.note: transliteration: Nyoliwala this side, baby. 'this side' = present/claiming presence.
